scope.label: flow-pack
scope.note: Second-stage audit decomposing the 33↔37 baseline gap and gate effectiveness. "FPAT-looking" membership is a title/branch heuristic — candidates need human confirmation and are never auto-labeled. The Phase-1 flow-pack filter is not adjusted.

gateFalseNegatives.note: Each count is contract-violating work that merged to main despite the existing gates (fpat-validate et al.) — i.e. false negatives of the current automation, sourced from the planning-accuracy report. Includes pre-FPAT-era items; see that report for the lists.

findings: Label coverage: 1 FPAT-looking issues + 1 FPAT-looking PRs lack the flow-pack label (heuristic candidates).
Taxonomy gaps on flow-pack items: 2 issues + 2 PRs missing type:/phase:/area: labels.
Run noise: cancelled 1 superseded vs 1 not; skipped 1 superseded vs 1 not.
Gate false negatives (merged despite contracts): branch 1, linkage 2, commit 1.
inferred: Every fpatLookingMissingFlowPack candidate is heuristic — whether it SHOULD carry flow-pack needs human confirmation.
A superseded cancelled run is benign-LOOKING noise; supersession by workflow+event is an approximation of same-trigger re-runs.
proven: Label coverage and gate effectiveness are quantifiable from durable GitHub state plus the stage-1 reports.
The 33↔37 baseline gap decomposes into noise vs missing-label vs definitional categories (see throughput-mismatch-analysis.md).
notProven: That any flagged FPAT-looking item should have carried flow-pack (heuristic, needs human confirmation).
Whether FPAT IMPROVED delivery — single baseline (n=1), no comparison cohort.
